articles.scope: Fire pump installation — power source (695.3), supply continuity, fire pump controller (695.7), conductor sizing (695.6), feeder location (695.6(B)), disconnect prohibition on the supply line (695.4).

key_sections.title: Power Source(s) for Electric Motor-Driven Fire Pumps
key_sections.summary: Power source either: (1) reliable utility service, (2) reliable on-site source (generator), (3) combination. 'Reliable' is the AHJ's call but generally means continuous availability

key_sections.title: Continuity of Power
key_sections.summary: Fire pump supply conductors shall be direct from the source of power to the fire pump controller (or a tap from the supply to non-fire-pump loads must not be subject to disconnection by any fire-related event)

key_sections.title: Transformers
key_sections.summary: Where a transformer feeds a fire pump, the transformer is to be sized at 125% of pump motor + 100% of associated equipment

key_sections.title: Power Wiring
key_sections.summary: Conductor ampacity ≥ 125% of fire pump motor + accessories. Single-conductor wiring methods may be used (not in raceway with non-fire-pump conductors)

key_sections.title: Voltage Drop
key_sections.summary: Voltage at fire pump controller terminals: shall not drop more than 15% below normal during motor starting; shall not drop more than 5% below 100% of normal during motor running

key_sections.title: Listed Equipment
key_sections.summary: Listed fire pump controller (NFPA 20-listed) and associated equipment

common_errors: Installing a disconnect on the fire pump supply line (other than the listed fire pump controller's disconnect) — prohibited by 695.4(B)(1)
Routing fire pump supply through normal panelboards or feeders subject to opening on a fire-floor signal — 695.4(B) requires the supply path to remain energised even on fire
Specifying voltage drop > 15% at motor start or > 5% at run — fire pump may stall or trip
Using a non-listed (non-NFPA 20) controller — NEC 695.10 requires listed equipment
articles.divergence_notes: Fire pump rules are NFPA-system-specific (NFPA 20 + NEC 695). IEC handles life-safety pumps via 60364-5-56 + national fire codes. NEC voltage-drop limits at fire pump (15% start, 5% run) are explicit; IEC defers to system-design coordination.
articles.usage_notes: Coordinate with the fire-protection engineer (NFPA 20 lead). Fire pump electrical and mechanical systems are tightly integrated — single-source design responsibility ideal.
